sharedRootCause: A value produced and shipped by the backend was wired into a frontend render path that never (or not always) executes, so the API carried the correct value while the operator never saw it. Occurrence 2 (WS3 F9): the executive-summary readiness mirror (es.readiness.summaryLine) was rendered only inside OverviewSummary, but OverviewTab's record-bearing branch — the branch every reconciled card takes — did not render OverviewSummary at all, so the readiness line existed in every API payload and on zero screens. The shared cause across occurrences: builders verify 'the payload contains X' and 'the component renders X', but never verify 'the component that renders X is itself reachable on the card states operators actually have'.
whyAutomatedTestsMissedIt: Source-scan UI tests asserted that OverviewSummary exists and that the readiness line is rendered by it, but no test asserted the RECORD-BEARING branch of OverviewTab (the dominant real-world branch) includes OverviewSummary. Payload tests asserted executiveSummary.readiness is present in the API. Nothing connected payload presence to render-path reachability.
whyBrowserQaMissedIt: It did not miss it — the WS3 independent browser QA caught it (F9) by comparing the API payload against every visible surface. Earlier occurrences were missed because QA journeys verified visible values matched the API, but did not enumerate API values that were VISIBLE NOWHERE.
missingInvariant: Every operator-facing value the backend ships in the canonical report payload must have at least one reachable render site on the card states operators actually encounter (record-bearing and record-less). A value rendered only in an unreachable branch is a missing value.
missingAcceptanceJourney: For each new payload field added for operators: open a record-bearing card in the browser and confirm the field is visible somewhere; also confirm on a record-less card where applicable. Browser QA must reconcile payload-to-screen in BOTH directions (screen values exist in API; operator-relevant API values appear on screen).
sharedRepair: OverviewTab's record branch now renders OverviewSummary first, so the executive summary and its shared readiness line are visible on every Overview. The unified readiness strip renders on all five readiness surfaces from ONE state object, which removes per-branch wiring as a failure point for readiness values.
newRegressionTest: readiness-surfaces-regression.test.ts (F9 block): asserts the record-bearing branch of OverviewTab contains <OverviewSummary>, that OverviewSummary renders es.readiness.summaryLine, and that the UnifiedReadinessStrip appears on at least five tab surfaces — reachability, not just existence.
newBrowserAssertion: On a record-bearing card, the Overview shows the executive summary block including the shared readiness summary line, and the same line matches unifiedReadiness.summaryLine from the authenticated API; the strip is present on Overview, Market, Strategy, Seller, and Reports.
